rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Where do you have to go ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I have to go to school.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: When do you have to go ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I have to go at ten O'clock.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Then, you have to read poetry now.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: No, I have to write the lesson now.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: beeda, niinu iiga padya oodleebeeku ; aameele paatha bariiboodu.
rows.english: No, you must read poetry new ; later on you may write the lesson.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Goodness. I  just cannot bear the sight of poetry.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Should I go to the market ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, you must go to the market to-day.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: What should I bring from there ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Vegetables and flowers should be brought.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: mithaayi tarbeedi, adanna kandre nange aagalla.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: oohoo, taraboodu ; haage dhoobiige batte kodabeeku.
rows.english: Oh, yes.   You may bring fruits.  On the way you should handover the clothes to the dhobi.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Ramu, you should wake up early in the morning tomorrow.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: What should I do when I wake up ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: You should bathe ; and then worship.
